Serialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"camelCase\")]\nstruct TasksListResponse {\n    results: Vec<AggregatedTask>,\n    limit: usize,\n    from: Option<u32>,\n    total: u32,\n}\n\n/// GET /tasks - List all tasks with optional filters.\nasync fn list_tasks(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Query(query): Query<TasksQuery>,\n) -> Result<Json<TasksListResponse>, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n    let limit = query.limit.unwrap_or(20);\n    let from = query.from;\n\n    // Query all nodes for tasks\n    let mut all_tasks: Vec<NodeTask> = Vec::new();\n    let mut failed_nodes = 0;\n\n    for group in topology.groups() {\n        if let Some(node_id) = group.nodes().first() {\n            let request = ScatterRequest {\n                method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n                path: \"/tasks\".to_string(),\n                body: vec![],\n                headers: vec![],\n            };\n\n            let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n\n            match scatter\n                .scatter(&topology, vec![node_id.clone()], request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n                .await\n            {\n                Ok(result) => {\n                    if let Some(resp) = result.responses.first() {\n                        if resp.status == 200 {\n                            if let Ok(json) = serde_json::from_slice::<serde_json::Value>(&resp.body) {\n                                if let Some(results) = json.get(\"results\").and_then(|r| r.as_array()) {\n                                    for task_value in results {\n                                        if let Ok(task) = serde_json::from_value::<NodeTask>(task_value.clone()) {\n                                            all_tasks.push(task);\n                                        }\n                                    }\n                                }\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Err(_) => {\n                    failed_nodes += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Apply filters if provided\n    let mut filtered_tasks: Vec<NodeTask> = all_tasks;\n\n    if let Some(index_uids) = &query.index_uid {\n        if !index_uids.is_empty() {\n            filtered_tasks = filtered_tasks\n                .into_iter()\n                .filter(|t| index_uids.contains(&t.index_uid))\n                .collect();\n        }\n    }\n\n    if let Some(statuses) = &query.statuses {\n        if !statuses.is_empty() {\n            filtered_tasks = filtered_tasks\n                .into_iter()\n                .filter(|t| statuses.contains(&t.status))\n                .collect();\n        }\n    }\n\n    if let Some(types) = &query.types {\n        if !types.is_empty() {\n            filtered_tasks = filtered_tasks\n                .into_iter()\n                .filter(|t| types.contains(&t.task_type))\n                .collect();\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Aggregate tasks by UID\n    let mut aggregated: std::collections::HashMap<u32, Vec<NodeTask>> = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n    for task in filtered_tasks {\n        aggregated\n            .entry(task.task_uid as u32)\n            .or_insert_with(Vec::new)\n            .push(task);\n    }\n\n    // Convert to aggregated tasks\n    let mut results: Vec<AggregatedTask> = aggregated\n        .into_iter()\n        .map(|(uid, tasks)| {\n            let first = tasks.first().unwrap();\n\n            // Determine overall status\n            let status = if tasks.iter().any(|t| t.status == \"failed\") {\n                \"failed\".to_string()\n            } else if tasks.iter().any(|t| t.status == \"processing\") {\n                \"processing\".to_string()\n            } else if tasks.iter().any(|t| t.status == \"enqueued\") {\n                \"enqueued\".to_string()\n            } else {\n                \"succeeded\".to_string()\n            };\n\n            let nodes_completed = tasks.iter().filter(|t| t.status == \"succeeded\").count() as u32;\n            let nodes_failed = tasks.iter().filter(|t| t.status == \"failed\").count() as u32;\n\n            AggregatedTask {\n                task_uid: uid as u64,\n                index_uid: first.index_uid.clone(),\n                status,\n                task_type: first.task_type.clone(),\n                enqueued_at: first.enqueued_at.clone(),\n                started_at: first.started_at.clone(),\n                finished_at: first.finished_at.clone(),\n                error: first.error.clone(),\n                details: first.details.clone(),\n                duration: first.duration.clone(),\n                node_count: tasks.len() as u32,\n                nodes_completed,\n                nodes_failed,\n            }\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    // Sort by task UID descending\n    results.sort_by(|a, b| b.task_uid.cmp(&a.task_uid));\n\n    // Apply from/limit pagination\n    let total = results.len() as u32;\n    if let Some(from_uid) = from {\n        results = results.into_iter().filter(|t| t.task_uid <= from_uid as u64).collect();\n    }\n    results.truncate(limit);\n\n    Ok(Json(TasksListResponse {\n        results,\n        limit,\n        from,\n        total,\n    }))\n}\n\n/// GET /tasks/:uid - Get a specific task.\nasync fn get_task(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(uid): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n\n    // Parse task UID\n    let task_uid = uid\n        .parse::<u64>()\n        .map_err(|_| ErrorResponse::invalid_request(\"Invalid task UID\"))?;\n\n    // Query all nodes for this task\n    let mut node_tasks: Vec<NodeTask> = Vec::new();\n    let mut not_found = true;\n\n    for group in topology.groups() {\n        if let Some(node_id) = group.nodes().first() {\n            let request = ScatterRequest {\n                method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n                path: format!(\"/tasks/{}\", task_uid),\n                body: vec![],\n                headers: vec![],\n            };\n\n            let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n\n            if let Ok(result) = scatter\n                .scatter(&topology, vec![node_id.clone()], request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n                .await\n            {\n                if let Some(resp) = result.responses.first() {\n                    if resp.status == 200 {\n                        not_found = false;\n                        if let Ok(json) = serde_json::from_slice::<serde_json::Value>(&resp.body) {\n                            if let Ok(task) = serde_json::from_value::<NodeTask>(json) {\n                                node_tasks.push(task);\n                            }\n                        }\n                    } else if resp.status == 404 {\n                        // Task not found on this node\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    if not_found {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::invalid_request(format!(\"Task {} not found\", uid)));\n    }\n\n    if node_tasks.is_empty() {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::invalid_request(format!(\"Task {} not found\", uid)));\n    }\n\n    // Aggregate task status\n    let first = node_tasks.first().unwrap();\n    let status = if node_tasks.iter().any(|t| t.status == \"failed\") {\n        \"failed\".to_string()\n    } else if node_tasks.iter().any(|t| t.status == \"processing\") {\n        \"processing\".to_string()\n    } else if node_tasks.iter().any(|t| t.status == \"enqueued\") {\n        \"enqueued\".to_string()\n    } else {\n        \"succeeded\".to_string()\n    };\n\n    let nodes_completed = node_tasks.iter().filter(|t| t.status == \"succeeded\").count() as u32;\n    let nodes_failed = node_tasks.iter().filter(|t| t.status == \"failed\").count() as u32;\n\n    let aggregated = AggregatedTask {\n        task_uid: first.task_uid,\n        index_uid: first.index_uid.clone(),\n        status,\n        task_type: first.task_type.clone(),\n        enqueued_at: first.enqueued_at.clone(),\n        started_at: first.started_at.clone(),\n        finished_at: first.finished_at.clone(),\n        error: first.error.clone(),\n        details: first.details.clone(),\n        duration: first.duration.clone(),\n        node_count: node_tasks.len() as u32,\n        nodes_completed,\n        nodes_failed,\n    };\n\n    Ok((axum::http::StatusCode::OK, Json(aggregated)).into_response())\n}\n\n/// DELETE /tasks/:uid - Cancel/delete a task.\nasync fn delete_task(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    Path(uid): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n\n    // Parse task UID\n    let task_uid = uid\n        .parse::<u64>()\n        .map_err(|_| ErrorResponse::invalid_request(\"Invalid task UID\"))?;\n\n    // Broadcast delete to all nodes\n    let targets = write_targets(0, &topology);\n\n    if targets.is_empty() {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(\"No nodes available\"));\n    }\n\n    let request = ScatterRequest {\n        method: \"DELETE\".to_string(),\n        path: format!(\"/tasks/{}\", task_uid),\n        body: vec![],\n        headers: vec![],\n    };\n\n    let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n    let result = scatter\n        .scatter(&topology, targets, request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n        .await\n        .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n\n    if let Some(resp) = result.responses.first() {\n        let status = Implements rules 2-5 for master-key/admin-key bearer dispatch:\n4\t//! - Rule 2: master-key can access all endpoints (full admin access)\n5\t//! - Rule 3: admin-key can access admin-only endpoints (/admin/*, /_miroir/*)\n6\t//! - Rule 4: No bearer token → public endpoints only (/health, /version)\n7\t//! - Rule 5: Invalid token → 403 Forbidden\n8\t\n9\tuse axum::{\n10\t    extract::State,\n11\t    http::HeaderMap,\n12\t    middleware::Next,\n13\t    response::Response,\n14\t};\n15\tuse crate::state::ProxyState;\n16\tuse crate::error_response::ErrorResponse;\n17\t\n18\t/// Token kind determined from the bearer token.\n19\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n20\tpub enum TokenKind {\n21\t    /// Master key - full access to all endpoints.\n22\t    Master,\n23\t    /// Admin key - access to admin endpoints only.\n24\t    Admin,\n25\t}\n26\t\n27\t/// Authentication result from bearer token validation.\n28\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n29\tpub enum AuthResult {\n30\t    /// Valid token with its kind.\n31\t    Valid(TokenKind),\n32\t    /// No bearer token present.\n33\t    None,\n34\t    /// Invalid bearer token.\n35\t    Invalid,\n36\t}\n37\t\n38\t/// Validate bearer token against the configured keys.\n39\tpub fn validate_bearer_token(headers: &HeaderMap, state: &ProxyState) -> AuthResult {\n40\t    let auth_header = match headers.get(\"authorization\") {\n41\t        Some(h) => h,\n42\t        None => return AuthResult::None,\n43\t    };\n44\t\n45\t    let auth_str = match auth_header.to_str() {\n46\t        Ok(s) => s,\n47\t        Err(_) => return AuthResult::Invalid,\n48\t    };\n49\t\n50\t    let token = match auth_str.strip_prefix(\"Bearer \") {\n51\t        Some(t) => t,\n52\t        None => return Bearer-token dispatch per plan §5\n//!\n//! Implements rules 2-5 for master-key/admin-key bearer dispatch:\n//! - Rule 2: master-key can access all endpoints (full admin access)\n//! - Rule 3: admin-key can access admin-only endpoints (/admin/*, /_miroir/*)\n//! - Rule 4: No bearer token → public endpoints only (/health, /version)\n//! - Rule 5: Invalid token → 403 Forbidden\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::State,\n    http::HeaderMap,\n    middleware::Next,\n    response::Response,\n};\nuse crate::state::ProxyState;\nuse crate::error_response::ErrorResponse;\n\n/// Token kind determined from the bearer token.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum TokenKind {\n    /// Master key - full access to all endpoints.\n    Master,\n    /// Admin key - access to admin endpoints only.\n    Admin,\n}\n\n/// Authentication result from bearer token validation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum AuthResult {\n    /// Valid token with its kind.\n    Valid(TokenKind),\n    /// No bearer token present.\n    None,\n    /// Invalid bearer token.\n    Invalid,\n}\n\n/// Validate bearer token against the configured keys.\npub fn validate_bearer_token(headers: &HeaderMap, state: &ProxyState) -> AuthResult {\n    let auth_header = match headers.get(\"authorization\") {\n        Some(h) => h,\n        None => return AuthResult::None,\n    };\n\n    let auth_str = match auth_header.to_str() {\n        Ok(s) => s,\n        Err(_) => return AuthResult::Invalid,\n    };\n\n    let token = match auth_str.strip_prefix(\"Bearer \") {\n        Some(t) => t,\n        None => return AuthResult::None,\n    };\n\n    // Check master key first (rule 2)\n    if state.is_valid_master_key(token) {\n        return AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Master);\n    }\n\n    // Check admin key (rule 3)\n    if state.is_valid_admin_key(token) {\n        return AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Admin);\n    }\n\n    // Invalid token (rule 5)\n    AuthResult::Invalid\n}\n\n/// Check if a path requires authentication.\npub fn requires_auth(path: &str) -> bool {\n    // Public endpoints (rule 4)\n    if path == \"/health\" || path == \"/version\" {\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    true\n}\n\n/// Check if a path requires admin access.\npub fn requires_admin(path: &str) -> bool {\n    // Admin endpoints (rule 3)\n    if path.starts_with(\"/admin/\") || path.starts_with(\"/_miroir/\") {\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    // /metrics endpoint requires admin\n    if path == \"/metrics\" {\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    false\n}\n\n/// Authentication middleware.\n///\n/// Enforces bearer token validation per plan §5 rules 2-5.\npub async fn auth_middleware(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    req: axum::extract::Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    let path = req.uri().path();\n\n    // Check if authentication is required\n    if !requires_auth(path) {\n        return Ok(next.run(req).await);\n    }\n\n    // Validate bearer token\n    let auth_result = validate_bearer_token(req.headers(), &state);\n\n    match auth_result {\n        AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Master) => {\n            // Master key has full access (rule 2)\n            Ok(next.run(req).await)\n        }\n        AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Admin) => {\n            // Admin key can only access admin endpoints (rule 3)\n            if requires_admin(path) {\n                Ok(next.run(req).await)\n            } else {\n                Err(ErrorResponse::new(\n                    \"Admin key cannot access this endpoint. Use master key.\",\n                    \"invalid_api_key\",\n                ))\n            }\n        }\n        AuthResult::None => {\n            // No bearer token → 401 (rule 4)\n            Err(ErrorResponse::new(\n                \"Missing Authorization header\",\n                \"missing_authorization_header\",\n            ))\n        }\n        AuthResult::Invalid => {\n            // Invalid token → 403 (rule 5)\n            Err(ErrorResponse::new(\n                \"Invalid API key\",\n                \"invalid_api_key\",\n            ))\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig;\n    use miroir_core::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n\n    fn test_state() -> ProxyState {\n        let mut &Default::default(),\n            )),\n            query_seq: std::sync::Arc::new(std::sync::atomic::AtomicU64::new(0)),\n            master_key: std::sync::Arc::new(\"test-master-key\".to_string()),\n            admin_key: std::sync::Arc::new(\"test-admin-key\".to_string()),\n            metrics: std::sync::Arc::new(crate::middleware::Metrics::new()),\n            task_manager: std::sync::Arc::new(crate::task_manager::TaskManager::new()),\n            retry_cache: HeaderMap {\n        let mut headers = HeaderMap::new();\n        if let Some(t) = token {\n            headers.insert(\"authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {t}\").parse().unwrap());\n        }\n        headers\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_master_key() {\n        let state = test_state();\n        let headers = make_headers(Some(\"test-master-key\"));\n\n        let result = ErrorResponse {\n    fn into_response(self) -> Response {\n        let status = if self.code == \"miroir_no_quorum\" || self.code == \"miroir_shard_unavailable\" {\n            StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE\n        } else if self.code.contains(\"not_found\") {\n            StatusCode::NOT_FOUND\n        } else {\n            StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST\n        };\n\n        (status, Json(self)).into_response()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Convert MiroirError to ErrorResponse.\nimpl From<miroir_core::MiroirError> for ErrorResponse {\n    fn from(err: miroir_core::MiroirError) -> Self {\n        match err {\n            miroir_core::MiroirError::Config(msg) => Self::new(msg, \"invalid_configuration\"),\n            miroir_core::MiroirError::Topology(msg) => Self::new(msg, \"invalid_topology\"),\n            miroir_core::MiroirError::Routing(msg) => Self::new(msg, \"internal_error\"),\n            miroir_core::MiroirError::Merge(msg) => Self::new(msg, \"internal_error\"),\n            miroir_core::MiroirError::Task(msg) => Self::new(msg, \"task_error\"),\n            miroir_core::MiroirError::Io(err) => Self::new(err.to_string(), \"internal_error\"),\n            miroir_core::MiroirError::Json(err) => Self::new(err.to_string(), \"internal_error\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n","numLines":141,"startLine":1,"totalLines":141}}}

NodeClient,\n    node_timeout_ms: u64,\n    /// Retry cache for idempotency (plan §4).\n    retry_cache: Option<RetryCache>,\n}\n\nimpl HttpScatter {\n    pub fn new(client: NodeClient, node_timeout_ms: u64) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            client,\n            node_timeout_ms,\n            retry_cache: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new HttpScatter with retry cache enabled.\n    pub fn with_retry_cache(client: NodeClient, node_timeout_ms: u64, retry_cache: RetryCache) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            client,\n            node_timeout_ms,\n            retry_cache: Some(retry_cache),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Send a request to a single node with retry cache lookup.\n    async fn send_to_node_with_cache(\n        &self,\n        topology: &Topology,\n        node_id: &NodeId,\n        method: &str,\n        path: &str,\n        body: &[u8],\n        headers: &[(String, String)],\n    ) -> Result<miroir_core::scatter::NodeResponse> {\n        // Check retry cache first if enabled\n        if let Some(ref cache) = self.retry_cache {\n            let cache_key = RetryCache::cache_key(body, node_id.as_str(), None);\n\n            if let Some(cached) = cache.get(&cache_key).await {\n                // Return cached response\n                return Ok(NodeResponse {\n                    node_id: node_id.clone(),\n                    body: cached.body,\n                    status: cached.status,\n                    headers: Vec::new(),\n                });\n            }\n        }\n\n        // No cache hit, send actual request\n        let timeout_dur = Duration::from_millis(self.node_timeout_ms);\n        let result = timeout(timeout_dur, async {\n            self.client\n                .send_to_node(topology, node_id, method, path, Some(body), headers)\n                .await\n        })\n        .await;\n\n        match result {\n            Ok(Ok(resp)) => {\n                let body_bytes = resp.body;\n                let status = resp.status;\n\n                // Cache successful responses\n                if let Some(ref cache) = self.retry_cache {\n                    // Cache only 2xx responses\n                    if (200..300).contains(&status) {\n                        let cache_key = RetryCache::cache_key(body, node_id.as_str(), None);\n                        let cached = CachedResponse {\n                            body: body_bytes.clone(),\n                            status,\n                            cached_at: std::time::Instant::now(),\n                        };\n                        cache.put(cache_key, cached).await;\n                    }\n                }\n\n                Ok(miroir_core::scatter::NodeResponse {\n                    node_id: node_id.clone(),\n                    body: body_bytes,\n                    status,\n                    headers: resp.headers,\n                })\n            }\n            _ => Err(MiroirError::Routing(format!(\n                \"request to node {} timed out\",\n                node_id.as_str()\n            ))),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl Scatter for HttpScatter {\n    async fn scatter(\n        &self,\n        topology: &Topology,\n        nodes: Vec<NodeId>,\n        request: ScatterRequest,\n        policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n    ) -> Result<ScatterResponse> {\n        // Fan out requests to all nodes in parallel\n        let futures: Vec<_> = nodes\n            .iter()\n            .map(|node_id| {\n                let node_id = node_id.clone();\n                let method = request.method.clone();\n                let path = request.path.clone();\n                let body = request.body.clone();\n                let headers = request.headers.clone();\n                let this = self.clone();\n                let topo = topology.clone();\n\n                async move {\n                    match this.send_to_node_with_cache(\n                        &topo,\n                        &node_id,\n                        &method,\n                        &path,\n                        &body,\n                        &headers,\n                    )\n                    .await\n                    {\n                        Ok(resp) => Ok(resp),\n                        Err(_) => Err(node_id),\n                    }\n                }\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        let results = futures::future::join_all(futures).await;\n\n        let mut successful = Vec::new();\n        let mut failed = Vec::new();\n\n        for result in results {\n            match result {\n                Ok(resp) => successful.push(resp),\n                Err(node_id) => failed.push(node_id),\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Check if we should fail based on policy\n        if policy == UnavailableShardPolicy::Error && !failed.is_empty() {\n            return Err(MiroirError::Routing(format!(\n                \"{} nodes failed: {:?}\",\n                failed.len(),\n                failed\n            )));\n        }\n\n        Ok(ScatterResponse {\n            responses: successful,\n            failed,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use miroir_core::topology::{Node, Topology};\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_http_scatter_empty_nodes() {\n        let client = NodeClient::new(\"test-key\".to_string(), &Default::default());\n        let scatter = HttpScatter::new(client, 1000);\n        let topology = Topology::new(64, 1);\n\n        let request = ScatterRequest {\n            body: Vec::new(),\n            headers: Vec::new(),\n            method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n            path: \"/\".to_string(),\n        };\n\n        let result = scatter\n            .scatter(&topology, Vec::new(), request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.responses.is_empty());\n        assert!(result.failed.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_http_scatter_timeout_handling() {\n        let client = NodeClient::new(\"test-key\".to_string(), &Default::default());\n        let scatter = HttpScatter::new(client, 1); // 1ms timeout\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 1);\n\n        // Add a node that will timeout\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"test-node\".to_string()),\n            \"http://invalid.example.com:7700\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n\n        let request = ScatterRequest {\n            body: Vec::new(),\n            headers: Vec::new(),\n            method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n            path: \"/\".to_string(),\n        };\n\n        let result = scatter\n            .scatter(\n                &topology,\n                vec![NodeId::new(\"test-node\".to_string())],\n                request,\n                UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n            )\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.responses.is_empty());\n        assert_eq!(result.failed.len(), 1);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_http_scatter_error_policy() {\n        let client = NodeClient::new(\"test-key\".to_string(), &Default::default());\n        let scatter = HttpScatter::new(client, 1);\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 1);\n\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"test-node\".to_string()),\n            \"http://invalid.example.com:7700\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n\n        let request = ScatterRequest {\n            body: Vec::new(),\n            headers: Vec::new(),\n vec![NodeId::new(\"test-node\".to_string())],\n                request,\n                UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n            )\n            .await;\n\n        assert!(result.is_err());\n    }\n}\n","numLines":267,"startLine":1,"totalLines":267}}}

miroir_core::topology::{NodeId, Topology};\nuse miroir_core::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse reqwest::Client;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::Duration;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n/// Node response with status code and body (as Vec<u8> for scatter compatibility).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct NodeResponse {\n    pub #[allow(dead_code)]\n    pub fn body_json(&self) -> Result<Value> {\n        serde_json::from_slice(&self.body).map_err(|e| MiroirError::Json(e))\n    }\n}\n\n/// HTTP client for scatter-gather requests to Meilisearch nodes.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct NodeClient {\n    /// Reqwest client with connection pooling.\n    client: Client,\n\n    /// Node master key for authentication.\n    node_master_key: Arc<String>,\n\n    /// Orchestrator-side retry cache for idempotency.\n    /// Key: sha256(request_body || target_node || idempotency_key)\n    /// Value: cached terminal response\n    retry_cache: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, CachedResponse>>>,\n}\n\n/// Cached response for retry deduplication.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\nstruct CachedResponse {\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    response: NodeResponse,\n    cached_at: std::time::Instant,\n}\n\nimpl NodeClient {\n    /// Create a new node client with connection pooling.\n    pub fn new(node_master_key: String, server_config: &ServerConfig) -> Self {\n        let client = Client::builder()\n            .pool_max_idle_per_host(32)\n            .pool_idle_timeout(Duration::from_secs(60))\n            .timeout(Duration::from_millis(server_config.request_timeout_ms))\n            .build()\n            .expect(\"failed to create HTTP client\");\n\n        Self {\n            client,\n            node_master_key: Arc::new(node_master_key),\n            retry_cache: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Send a request to a single node.\n    pub async fn send_to_node(\n        &self,\n        topology: &Topology,\n        node_id: &NodeId,\n        method: &str,\n        path: &str,\n        body: Option<&[u8]>,\n        headers: &[(String, String)],\n    ) -> Result<NodeResponse> {\n        let node = topology\n            .node(node_id)\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"node {} not found\", node_id.as_str())))?;\n\n        let url = format!(\"{}{}\", node.address, path);\n\n        let mut request = match method {\n            \"GET\" => self.client.get(&url),\n            \"POST\" => self.client.post(&url),\n            \"PUT\" => self.client.put(&url),\n            \"PATCH\" => self.client.patch(&url),\n            \"DELETE\" => self.client.delete(&url),\n            _ => {\n                return Err(MiroirError::Routing(format!(\n                    \"unsupported HTTP method: {method}\",\n                )))\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Add node master key header\n        request = request.header(\n            \"Authorization\",\n            format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.node_master_key.as_str()),\n        );\n\n        // Add custom headers\n        for (key, value) in headers {\n            request = request.header(key, value);\n        }\n\n        // Add body if present\n        if let Some(body_bytes) = body {\n            request = request.header(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\");\n            request = request.body(body_bytes.to_vec());\n        }\n\n        let response = request.send().await.map_err(|e| {\n            MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"request to node {} failed: {e}\", node_id.as_str(),))\n        })?;\n\n        let status = response.status().as_u16();\n\n        // Collect response headers\n        let response_headers: Vec<(String, String)> = response\n            .headers()\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|(name, value)| {\n                value\n                    .to_str()\n                    .ok()\n                    .map(|v| (name.as_str().to_string(), v.to_string()))\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        let body_bytes = response\n            .bytes()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"failed to read response body: {e}\")))?;\n\n        Ok(NodeResponse {\n            node_id: node_id.clone(),\n            status,\n            body: body_bytes.to_vec(),\n            headers: response_headers,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Send requests to multiple nodes in parallel.\n    pub async fn send_to_many(\n        &self,\n        topology: &Topology,\n        node_ids: &[NodeId],\n        method: &str,\n        path: &str,\n        body: Option<&[u8]>,\n        headers: &[(String, String)],\n    ) -> Vec<Result<NodeResponse>> {\n        let futures: Vec<_> = node_ids\n            .iter()\n            .map(|node_id| self.send_to_node(topology, node_id, method, path, body, headers))\n            .collect();\n\n        futures::future::join_all(futures).await\n    }\n\n    /// Prune old entries from the retry cache.\n    pub async fn prune_retry_cache(&self, max_age: Duration) {\n        let mut cache = self.retry_cache.write().await;\n        let now = std::time::Instant::now();\n        cache.retain(|_, entry| now.duration_since(entry.cached_at) < max_age);\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_d2a36721b71e425aa6a2f997","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Orchestrator-side retry cache for idempotency (plan §4).\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Key: sha256(batch || target_node || idempotency_key_or_mtask)\n4\t//!\n5\t//! This cache prevents duplicate writes when retrying timed-out requests\n6\t//! to nodes. It stores terminal responses (success or definitive failure)\n7\t//! for a configurable TTL.\n8\t\n9\tuse sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\n10\tuse std::collections::HashMap;\n11\tuse std::sync::Arc;\n12\tuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\n13\tuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n14\t\n15\t/// Cached response from a node.\n16\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n17\tpub struct CachedResponse {\n18\t    /// Response body.\n19\t    pub body: Vec<u8>,\n20\t    /// HTTP status code.\n21\t    pub status: u16,\n22\t    /// When this cache entry was created.\n23\t    pub cached_at: Instant,\n24\t}\n25\t\n26\timpl CachedResponse {\n27\t    /// Check if this entry is still valid (not expired).\n28\t    pub fn is_valid(&self, ttl: Duration) -> bool {\n29\t        self.cached_at.elapsed() < ttl\n30\t    }\n31\t}\n32\t\n33\t/// Retry cache for idempotency.\n34\t#[derive(Clone)]\n35\tpub struct RetryCache {\n36\t    entries: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, CachedResponse>>>,\n37\t    default_ttl: Duration,\n38\t}\n39\t\n40\timpl RetryCache {\n41\t    /// Create a new retry cache with the specified default TTL.\n42\t    pub fn new(default_ttl: Duration) -> Self {\n43\t        Self {\n44\t            entries: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n45\t            default_ttl,\n46\t        }\n47\t    }\n48\t\n49\t    /// Generate cache key from request components.\n50\t    ///\n51\t    /// Key format: sha256(batch || target_node || idempotency_key_or_mtask)\n52\t    pub fn cache_key(\n53\t        batch: &[u8],\n54\t        target_node: &str,\n55\t        idempotency_key: Option<&str>,\n56\t    ) -> String {\n57\t        let mut hasher = Sha256::new();\n58\t\n59\t        // Include batch\n60\t        hasher.update(batch);\n61\t\n62\t        // Include target node\n63\t        hasher.update(target_node.as_bytes());\n64\t\n65\t        // Include idempotency key or use empty string\n66\t        if let Some(key) = idempotency_key {\n67\t            hasher.update(key.as_bytes());\n68\t        }\n69\t\n70\t        let result = hasher.finalize();\n71\t        hex::encode(result)\n72\t    }\n73\t\n74\t    /// Get a cached response if it exists and is still valid.\n75\t    pub async fn get(&self, key: &str) -> Option<CachedResponse> {\n76\t        let cache = self.entries.read().await;\n77\t        cache.get(key).and_then(|entry| {\n78\t            if entry.is_valid(self.default_ttl) {\n79\t                Some(entry.clone())\n80\t            } else {\n81\t                None\n82\t            }\n83\t        })\n84\t    }\n85\t\n86\t    /// Store a response in the cache.\n87\t    pub async fn put(&self, key: String, response: CachedResponse) {\n88\t        let mut cache = self.entries.write().await;\n89\t        cache.insert(key, response);\n90\t    }\n91\t\n92\t    /// Remove expired entries from the cache.\n93\t    pub async fn prune(&self) {\n94\t        let mut cache = self.entries.write().await;\n95\t        let now = Instant::now();\n96\t\n97\t        cache.retain(|_, entry| {\n98\t            now.duration_since(entry.cached_at) < self.default_ttl\n99\t        });\n100\t    }\n101\t\n102\t    /// Clear all entries from the cache.\n103\t    pub async fn clear(&self) {\n104\t        let mut cache = self.entries.write().await;\n105\t        cache.clear();\n106\t    }\n107\t\n108\t    /// Get the number of entries in the cache.\n109\t    pub async fn len(&self) -> usize {\n110\t        let cache = self.entries.read().await;\n111\t        cache.len()\n112\t    }\n113\t\n114\t    /// Check if the cache is empty.\n115\t    pub async fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {\n116\t        let cache = self.entries.read().await;\n117\t        cache.is_empty()\n118\t    }\n119\t}\n120\t\n121\t#[cfg(test)]\n122\tmod tests {\n123\t    use super::*;\n124\t\n125\t    #[test]\n126\t    fn test_cache_key_generation() {\n127\t        let batch = b\"test_batch\";\n128\t        let target_node = \"node1\";\n129\t        let idempotency_key = Some(\"key123\");\n130\t\n131\t        let key1 = RetryCache::cache_key(batch, target_node, idempotency_key);\n132\t        let key2 = RetryCache::cache_key(batch, target_node, idempotency_key);\n133\t\n134\t        // Same inputs should produce same key\n135\t        assert_eq!(key1, key2);\n136\t\n137\t        // Different inputs should produce different keys\n138\t        let key3 = RetryCache::cache_key(batch, \"node2\", idempotency_key);\n139\t        assert_ne!(key1, key3);\n140\t    }\n141\t\n142\t    #[test]\n143\t    fn test_cache_key_without_idempotency() {\n144\t        let batch = b\"test_batch\";\n145\t        let target_node = \"node1\";\n146\t\n147\t        let key1 = RetryCache::cache_key(batch, target_node, None);\n148\t        let key2 = Orchestrator-side retry cache for idempotency (plan §4).\n//!\n//! Key: sha256(batch || target_node || idempotency_key_or_mtask)\n//!\n//! This cache prevents duplicate writes when retrying timed-out requests\n//! to nodes. It stores terminal responses (success or definitive failure)\n//! for a configurable TTL.\n\nuse sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n/// Cached response from a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct CachedResponse {\n    /// Response body.\n    pub body: Vec<u8>,\n    /// HTTP status code.\n    pub status: u16,\n    /// When this cache entry was created.\n    pub cached_at: Instant,\n}\n\nimpl CachedResponse {\n    /// Check if this entry is still valid (not expired).\n    pub fn is_valid(&self, ttl: Duration) -> bool {\n        self.cached_at.elapsed() < ttl\n    }\n}\n\n/// Retry cache for idempotency.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct RetryCache {\n    entries: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, CachedResponse>>>,\n    default_ttl: Duration,\n}\n\nimpl RetryCache {\n    /// Create a new retry cache with the specified default TTL.\n    pub fn new(default_ttl: Duration) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            entries: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            default_ttl,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Generate cache key from request components.\n    ///\n    /// Key format: sha256(batch || target_node || idempotency_key_or_mtask)\n    pub fn cache_key(\n        batch: &[u8],\n        target_node: &str,\n        idempotency_key: Option<&str>,\n    ) -> String {\n        let mut hasher = Sha256::new();\n\n        // Include batch\n        hasher.update(batch);\n\n        // Include target node\n        hasher.update(target_node.as_bytes());\n\n        // Include idempotency key or use empty string\n        if let Some(key) = idempotency_key {\n            hasher.update(key.as_bytes());\n        }\n\n        let result = hasher.finalize();\n        hex::encode(result)\n    }\n\n    /// Get a cached response if it exists and is still valid.\n    pub async fn get(&self, key: &str) -> Option<CachedResponse> {\n        let cache = self.entries.read().await;\n        cache.get(key).and_then(|entry| {\n            if entry.is_valid(self.default_ttl) {\n                Some(entry.clone())\n            } else {\n                None\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Store a response in the cache.\n    pub async fn put(&self, key: String, response: CachedResponse) {\n        let mut cache = self.entries.write().await;\n        cache.insert(key, response);\n    }\n\n    /// Remove expired entries from the cache.\n    pub async fn prune(&self) {\n        let mut cache = self.entries.write().await;\n        let now = Instant::now();\n\n        cache.retain(|_, entry| {\n            now.duration_since(entry.cached_at) < self.default_ttl\n        });\n    }\n\n    /// Clear all entries from the cache.\n    pub async fn clear(&self) {\n        let mut cache = self.entries.write().await;\n        cache.clear();\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of entries in the cache.\n    pub async fn len(&self) -> usize {\n        let cache = self.entries.read().await;\n        cache.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the cache is empty.\n    pub async fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {\n        let cache = self.entries.read().await;\n        cache.is_empty()\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_cache_key_generation() {\n        let batch = b\"test_batch\";\n        let target_node = \"node1\";\n        let idempotency_key = Some(\"key123\");\n\n        let key1 = RetryCache::cache_key(batch, target_node, idempotency_key);\n        let key2 = RetryCache::cache_key(batch, target_node, idempotency_key);\n\n        // Same inputs should produce same key\n        assert_eq!(key1, key2);\n\n        // Different inputs should produce different keys\n        let key3 = RetryCache::cache_key(batch, \"node2\", idempotency_key);\n        assert_ne!(key1, key3);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_cache_key_without_idempotency() {\n        let batch = b\"test_batch\";\n        let target_node = \"node1\";\n\n        let key1 = RetryCache::cache_key(batch, target_node, None);\n        let key2 = RetryCache::cache_key(batch, target_node, None);\n\n        // Same inputs should produce same key\n        assert_eq!(key1, key2);\n\n        // With and without idempotency key should be different\n        let key3 = RetryCache::cache_key(batch, target_node, Some(\"key123\"));\n        assert_ne!(key1, key3);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_cache_put_get() {\n        let cache = RetryCache::new(Duration::from_secs(60));\n\n        let response = CachedResponse {\n            body: b\"test_response\".to_vec(),\n            status: 200,\n            cached_at: Instant::now(),\n        };\n\n        let key = \"test_key\".to_string();\n        cache.put(key.clone(), response.clone()).await;\n\n        let retrieved = cache.get(&key).await;\n        assert!(retrieved.is_some());\n        let retrieved = retrieved.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(retrieved.body, b\"test_response\");\n        assert_eq!(retrieved.status, 200);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_cache_expiration() {\n        let cache = RetryCache::new(Duration::from_millis(100));\n\n        let response = CachedResponse {\n            body: b\"test_response\".to_vec(),\n            status: 200,\n            cached_at: Instant::now(),\n        };\n\n        let key = \"test_key\".to_string();\n        cache.put(key.clone(), response).await;\n\n        // Should be valid immediately\n        let retrieved = cache.get(&key).await;\n        assert!(retrieved.is_some());\n\n        // Wait for expiration\n        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(150)).await;\n\n        // Should be expired now\n        let retrieved = cache.get(&key).await;\n        assert!(retrieved.is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_cache_prune() {\n        let cache = RetryCache::new(Duration::from_millis(100));\n\n        let response1 = CachedResponse {\n            body: b\"test_response1\".to_vec(),\n            status: 200,\n            cached_at: Instant::now(),\n        };\n\n        let response2 = CachedResponse {\n            body: b\"test_response2\".to_vec(),\n            status: 200,\n            cached_at: Instant::now(),\n        };\n\n        cache.put(\"key1\".to_string(), response1).await;\n        cache.put(\"key2\".to_string(), response2).await;\n\n        assert_eq!(cache.len().await, 2);\n\n        // Wait for expiration\n        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(150)).await;\n\n        cache.prune().await;\n\n        // Should be empty after pruning\n        assert_eq!(cache.len().await, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_cache_clear() {\n        let cache = RetryCache::new(Duration::from_secs(60));\n\n        let response = CachedResponse {\n            body: b\"test_response\".to_vec(),\n            status: 200,\n            cached_at: Instant::now(),\n        };\n\n        cache.put(\"key1\".to_string(), response.clone()).await;\n        cache.put(\"key2\".to_string(), response).await;\n\n        assert_eq!(cache.len().await, 2);\n\n        cache.clear().await;\n\n        assert_eq!(cache.len().await, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_cached_response_is_valid() {\n        let response = CachedResponse {\n            body: vec![],\n            status: 200,\n            cached_at: Instant::now(),\n        };\n\n        assert!(response.is_valid(Duration::from_secs(60)));\n assert!(cache.is_empty().await);\n\n        let response = CachedResponse {\n            body: vec![],\n            status: 200,\n            cached_at: Instant::now(),\n        };\n\n Task ID generation and reconciliation per plan §3.\n//!\n//! - Generates unique Miroir task IDs\n//! - Tracks node task UIDs for reconciliation\n//! - Aggregates task status across nodes\n\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU64, Ordering};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\n/// Task manager for generating and tracking tasks.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct TaskManager {\n    /// Next task UID (sequential for Meilisearch compatibility)\n    next_uid: Arc<AtomicU64>,\n}\n\nimpl TaskManager {\n    /// Create a new task manager.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            next_uid: Arc::new(AtomicU64::new(1)),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Generate a new task UID.\n    pub fn next_uid(&self) -> u64 {\n        self.next_uid.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst)\n    }\n\n    /// Generate a unique Miroir task ID (UUID-based).\n    pub fn generate_miroir_task_id(&self) -> String {\n        format!(\"mtask-{}\", Uuid::new_v4())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for TaskManager {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Task reconciliation state for tracking responses from nodes.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TaskReconciliation {\n    /// Miroir task ID.\n    pub miroir_task_id: String,\n    /// Task UID for client responses.\n    pub task_uid: u64,\n    /// Node task UIDs keyed by node ID.\n    pub node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>,\n    /// Which groups met quorum.\n    pub successful_groups: Vec<u32>,\n    /// Which groups missed quorum.\n    pub degraded_groups: Vec<u32>,\n}\n\nimpl TaskReconciliation {\n    /// Create a new task reconciliation state.\n    pub fn new(miroir_task_id: String, task_uid: u64) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            miroir_task_id,\n            task_uid,\n            node_tasks: HashMap::new(),\n            successful_groups: Vec::new(),\n            degraded_groups: Vec::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add a node task response.\n    pub fn add_node_task(&mut self, node_id: String, task_uid: u64) {\n        self.node_tasks.insert(node_id, task_uid);\n    }\n\n    /// Mark a group as successful (met quorum).\n    pub fn mark_group_success(&mut self, group_id: u32) {\n        if !self.successful_groups.contains(&group_id) {\n            self.successful_groups.push(group_id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Mark a group as degraded (missed quorum).\n    pub fn mark_group_degraded(&mut self, group_id: u32) {\n        if !self.degraded_groups.contains(&group_id) {\n            self.degraded_groups.push(group_id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the task is degraded (any group missed quorum).\n    pub fn is_degraded(&self) -> bool {\n        !self.degraded_groups.is_empty()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the task succeeded completely (all groups met quorum).\n    pub fn is_full_success(&self) -> bool {\n        self.degraded_groups.is_empty() && !self.successful_groups.is_empty()\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_task_manager_uid_generation() {\n        let manager = TaskManager::new();\n        let uid1 = manager.next_uid();\n        let uid2 = manager.next_uid();\n\n        assert_eq!(uid1, 1);\n        assert_eq!(uid2, 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn reconciliation.add_node_task(\"node2\".to_string(), 101);\n\n        assert_eq!(reconciliation.node_tasks.len(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(reconciliation.node_tasks.get(\"node1\"), Some(&100));\n reconciliation.mark_group_degraded(2);\n\n        assert_eq!(reconciliation.successful_groups, vec![0, 1]);\n        assert_eq!(reconciliation.degraded_groups, vec![2]);\n        assert!(reconciliation.is_degraded());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_task_reconciliation_full_success() {\n        let mut reconciliation = TaskReconciliation::new(\"mtask-123\".to_string(), Result merger: combines shard results into a single response.\n2\t\n3\tuse crate::Result;\n4\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n5\tuse serde_json::Value;\n6\tuse std::collections::{BTreeMap, BinaryHeap};\n7\t\n8\t/// Input to the merge function.\n9\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n10\tpub struct MergeInput {\n11\t    /// One page of hits per node in the covering set.\n12\t    pub shard_hits: Vec<ShardHitPage>,\n13\t\n14\t    /// Offset to apply after merge.\n15\t    pub offset: usize,\n16\t\n17\t    /// Limit to apply after merge.\n18\t    pub limit: usize,\n19\t\n20\t    /// Whether the client requested the ranking score in the response.\n21\t    pub client_requested_score: bool,\n22\t\n23\t    /// Facet names to include (if None, include all).\n24\t    pub facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n25\t}\n26\t\n27\t/// A page of hits from a single shard.\n28\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n29\tpub struct ShardHitPage {\n30\t    /// Raw JSON response from the node.\n31\t    pub body: Value,\n32\t\n33\t    /// Whether this shard succeeded.\n34\t    pub success: bool,\n35\t}\n36\t\n37\t/// Merged search result.\n38\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n39\tpub struct MergedSearchResult {\n40\t    /// Merged hits (globally sorted, offset/limit applied).\n41\t    pub hits: Vec<Value>,\n42\t\n43\t    /// Aggregated facets.\n44\t    pub facets: Value,\n45\t\n46\t    /// Estimated total hits (sum of shard totals).\n47\t    pub estimated_total_hits: u64,\n48\t\n49\t    /// Processing time in milliseconds.\n50\t    pub processing_time_ms: u64,\n51\t\n52\t    /// Whether the response is degraded (some shards failed).\n53\t    pub degraded: bool,\n54\t}\n55\t\n56\t/// Merge search results from multiple shards.\n57\t///\n58\t/// This is the primary entry point for result merging. It:\n59\t/// 1. Collects all hits with scores from all shards\n60\t/// 2. Sorts globally by `_rankingScore` descending\n61\t/// 3. Applies offset + limit after merge\n62\t/// 4. Strips `_rankingScore` if client didn't request it\n63\t/// 5. Always strips `_miroir_*` reserved fields\n64\t/// 6. Sums facet counts across shards\n65\t/// 7. Sums `estimatedTotalHits` across shards\n66\t/// 8. Takes max `processingTimeMs` across shards\n67\t///\n68\t/// Uses a binary min-heap to avoid keeping all hits in RAM when fan-out is large.\n69\t/// Uses BTreeMap for stable facet serialization (deterministic JSON output).\n70\tpub fn merge(input: MergeInput) -> MergedSearchResult {\n71\t    // Filter to only successful responses\n72\t    let successful_shards: Vec<_> = input\n73\t        .shard_hits\n74\t        .iter()\n75\t        .filter(|s| s.success)\n76\t        .collect();\n77\t\n78\t    // Check if any shards failed (degraded mode)\n79\t    let degraded = successful_shards.len() < input.shard_hits.len();\n80\t\n81\t    // Collect all hits with their ranking scores and primary keys\n82\t    let mut all_hits: Vec<HitWithScore> = Vec::new();\n83\t\n84\t    for shard in &successful_shards {\n85\t        if let Some(hits) = shard.body.get(\"hits\").and_then(|h| h.as_array()) {\n86\t            for hit in hits {\n87\t                let score = hit\n88\t                    .get(\"_rankingScore\")\n89\t                    .and_then(|s| s.as_f64())\n90\t                    .unwrap_or(0.0);\n91\t\n92\t                let primary_key = hit\n93\t                    .get(\"id\")\n94\t                    .and_then(|id| id.as_str())\n95\t                    .unwrap_or(\"\")\n96\t                    .to_string();\n97\t\n98\t                all_hits.push(HitWithScore {\n99\t                    score,\n100\t                    primary_key,\n101\t                    hit: hit.clone(),\n102\t                });\n103\t            }\n104\t        }\n105\t    }\n106\t\n107\t    // Use a min-heap of size offset + limit to avoid keeping all hits in RAM\n108\t    let heap_size = input.offset + input.limit;\n109\t    let top_hits = if all_hits.len() > heap_size * 2 {\n110\t        // Only use heap optimization if we have significantly more hits than needed\n111\t        // Use BinaryHeap with Reverse to get min-heap behavior\n112\t        // Reverse<T> makes the \"smallest\" T become the \"largest\" Reverse<T>\n113\t        // So in BinaryHeap (max-heap), the smallest T ends up at the top\n114\t        use std::cmp::Reverse;\n115\t\n116\t        // First, define a wrapper with natural ascending order by score\n117\t        #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n118\t        struct AscendingHit(HitWithScore);\n119\t\n120\t        impl PartialOrd for AscendingHit {\n121\t            fn partial_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Option<std::cmp::Ordering> {\n122\t                Some(self.cmp(other))\n123\t            }\n124\t        }\n125\t\n126\t        impl Ord for AscendingHit {\n127\t            fn cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> std::cmp::Ordering {\n128\t                // Ascending order: lower score first, then primary key ascending\n129\t                match self.0.score.partial_cmp(&other.0.score) {\n130\t                    Some(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal) | None => {\n131\t                        self.0.primary_key.cmp(&other.0.primary_key)\n132\t                    }\n133\t                    Some(ord) => ord,\n134\t                }\n135\t            }\n136\t        }\n137\t\n138\t        // Reverse<AscendingHit> in BinaryHeap gives us a min-heap by score\n139\t        // The lowest score (worst) will be at the top for easy replacement\n140\t        let mut heap: BinaryHeap<Reverse<AscendingHit>> = BinaryHeap::new();\n141\t\n142\t        for hit in all_hits {\n143\t            if heap.len() < heap_size {\n144\t                heap.push(Reverse(AscendingHit(hit)));\n145\t            } else {\n146\t                // Peek at the smallest (worst) hit in our top-k\n147\t                if let Some(Reverse(worst)) = heap.peek() {\n148\t                    // If current hit is better than our worst, replace it\n149\t                    // Compare using the score directly\n150\t                    if hit.score > worst.0.score ||\n151\t                       (hit.score == worst.0.score && hit.primary_key < worst.0.primary_key) {\n152\t                        heap.pop();\n153\t                        heap.push(Reverse(AscendingHit(hit)));\n154\t                    }\n155\t                }\n156\t            }\n157\t        }\n158\t\n159\t        // Convert to sorted vector (descending by score)\n160\t        let mut result: Vec<_> = heap.into_iter().map(|r| (r.0).0).collect();\n161\t        result.sort_by(|a, b| a.descending_cmp(b));\n162\t        result\n163\t    } else {\n164\t        // For smaller result sets, just sort directly\n165\t        all_hits.sort_by(|a, b| a.descending_cmp(b));\n166\t        all_hits\n167\t    };\n168\t\n169\t    // Apply offset and limit\n170\t    let page: Vec<Value> = top_hits\n171\t        .into_iter()\n172\t        .skip(input.offset)\n173\t        .take(input.limit)\n174\t        .map(|mut hit_with_score| {\n175\t            let hit = &mut hit_with_score.hit;\n176\t\n177\t            // Strip all _miroir_* fields (always removed)\n178\t            if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n179\t                obj.retain(|k, _| !k.starts_with(\"_miroir_\"));\n180\t            }\n181\t\n182\t            // Strip _rankingScore if client didn't request it\n183\t            if !input.client_requested_score {\n184\t                if let Some(obj) = hit.as_object_mut() {\n185\t                    obj.remove(\"_rankingScore\");\n186\t                }\n187\t            }\n188\t\n189\t            hit.clone()\n190\t        })\n191\t        .collect();\n192\t\n193\t    // Aggregate facets across all shards\n194\t    let facets = merge_facets(&successful_shards, input.facets.as_deref());\n195\t\n196\t    // Sum estimated total hits\n197\t    let estimated_total_hits: u64 = successful_shards\n198\t        .iter()\n199\t        .filter_map(|s| s.body.get(\"estimatedTotalHits\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))\n200\t        .sum();\n201\t\n202\t    // Max processing time across all shards\n203\t    let processing_time_ms: u64 = successful_shards\n204\t        .iter()\n205\t        .filter_map(|s| s.body.get(\"processingTimeMs\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()))\n206\t        .max()\n207\t        .unwrap_or(0);\n208\t\n209\t    MergedSearchResult {\n210\t        hits: page,\n211\t        facets,\n212\t        estimated_total_hits,\n213\t        processing_time_ms,\n214\t        degraded,\n215\t    }\n216\t}\n217\t\n218\t/// A hit with its ranking score for sorting.\n219\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n220\tstruct HitWithScore {\n221\t    score: f64,\n222\t    primary_key: String,\n223\t    hit: Value,\n224\t}\n225\t\n226\timpl PartialEq for HitWithScore {\n227\t    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool {\n228\t        self.score == other.score && self.primary_key == other.primary_key\n229\t    }\n230\t}\n231\t\n232\timpl Eq for HitWithScore {}\n233\t\n234\timpl PartialOrd for HitWithScore {\n235\t    fn partial_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> Option<std::cmp::Ordering> {\n236\t        Some(self.cmp(other))\n237\t    }\n238\t}\n239\t\n240\timpl HitWithScore {\n241\t    /// Compare for descending order (score desc, primary key asc).\n242\t    fn descending_cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> std::cmp::Ordering {\n243\t        // Sort by score descending, then by primary key ascending for tie-breaking\n244\t        match other.score.partial_cmp(&self.score) {\n245\t            Some(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal) | None => {\n246\t                // On equal scores (or NaN), fall back to primary key ascending\n247\t                self.primary_key.cmp(&other.primary_key)\n248\t            }\n249\t            Some(ord) => ord,\n250\t        }\n251\t    }\n252\t}\n253\t\n254\timpl Ord for HitWithScore {\n255\t    fn cmp(&self, other: &Self) -> std::cmp::Ordering {\n256\t        // Sort by score descending, then by primary key ascending for tie-breaking\n257\t        match self.score.partial_cmp(&other.score) {\n258\t            Some(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal) | None => {\n259\t                // On equal scores (or NaN), fall back to primary key\n260\t                // Reversed for descending order\n261\t                other.primary_key.cmp(&self.primary_key)\n262\t            }\n263\t            Some(ord) => ord.reverse(),\n264\t        }\n265\t    }\n266\t}\n267\t\n268\t/// Result merger: combines responses from multiple shards.\n269\tpub trait Merger: Send + Sync {\n270\t    /// Merge search results from multiple shards.\n271\t    ///\n272\t    /// Takes the raw JSON responses from each shard and produces\n273\t    /// a merged result with global sorting, offset/limit applied,\n274\t    /// and facet aggregation.\n275\t    fn merge(\n276\t        &self,\n277\t        shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n278\t        offset: usize,\n279\t        limit: usize,\n280\t        client_requested_score: bool,\n281\t    ) -> Result<MergedResult>;\n282\t}\n283\t\n284\t/// Response from a single shard.\n285\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n286\tpub struct ShardResponse {\n287\t    /// Shard identifier.\n288\t    pub shard_id: u32,\n289\t\n290\t    /// Raw JSON response from the node.\n291\t    pub body: Value,\n292\t\n293\t    /// Whether this shard succeeded.\n294\t    pub success: bool,\n295\t}\n296\t\n297\t/// Merged search result (legacy, use MergedSearchResult instead).\n298\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n299\tpub struct MergedResult {\n300\t    /// Merged hits (globally sorted, offset/limit applied).\n301\t    pub hits: Vec<Value>,\n302\t\n303\t    /// Aggregated facets.\n304\t    pub facets: Value,\n305\t\n306\t    /// Estimated total hits (sum of shard totals).\n307\t    pub total_hits: u64,\n308\t\n309\t    /// Processing time in milliseconds.\n310\t    pub processing_time_ms: u64,\n311\t\n312\t    /// Whether the response is degraded (some shards failed).\n313\t    pub degraded: bool,\n314\t}\n315\t\n316\t/// Default implementation of Merger.\n317\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n318\tpub struct MergerImpl;\n319\t\n320\timpl Merger for MergerImpl {\n321\t    fn merge(\n322\t        &self,\n323\t        shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n324\t        offset: usize,\n325\t        limit: usize,\n326\t        client_requested_score: bool,\n327\t    ) -> Result<MergedResult> {\n328\t        // Convert ShardResponse to ShardHitPage\n329\t        let shard_hits: Vec<ShardHitPage> = shard_responses\n330\t            .into_iter()\n331\t            .map(|sr| ShardHitPage {\n332\t                body: sr.body,\n333\t                success: sr.success,\n334\t            })\n335\t            .collect();\n336\t\n337\t        // Call the new merge function\n338\t        let input = MergeInput {\n339\t            shard_hits,\n340\t            offset,\n341\t            limit,\n342\t            client_requested_score,\n343\t            facets: None,\n344\t        };\n345\t\n346\t        let result = merge(input);\n347\t\n348\t        Ok(MergedResult {\n349\t            hits: result.hits,\n350\t            facets: result.facets,\n351\t            total_hits: result.estimated_total_hits,\n352\t            processing_time_ms: result.processing_time_ms,\n353\t            degraded: result.degraded,\n354\t        })\n355\t    }\n356\t}\n357\t\n358\t/// Merge facet distributions from all shards.\n359\t///\n360\t/// Facets are nested objects like `{\"color\": {\"red\": 10, \"blue\": 5}}`.\n361\t/// We sum counts for each facet value across all shards.\n362\t///\n363\t/// Uses BTreeMap for stable, deterministic serialization.\n364\tfn merge_facets(shards: &[&ShardHitPage], facet_filter: Option<&[String]>) -> Value {\n365\t    let mut merged_facets: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n366\t\n367\t    for shard in shards {\n368\t        if let Some(facets) = shard\n369\t            .body\n370\t            .get(\"facetDistribution\")\n371\t            .and_then(|f| f.as_object())\n372\t        {\n373\t            for (facet_name, facet_values) in facets {\n374\t                // Apply facet filter if provided\n375\t                if let Some(filter) = facet_filter {\n376\t                    if !filter.contains(&facet_name) {\n377\t                        continue;\n378\t                    }\n379\t                }\n380\t\n381\t                if let Some(values_obj) = facet_values.as_object() {\n382\t                    let entry = merged_facets.entry(facet_name.clone()).or_default();\n383\t\n384\t                    for (value, count) in values_obj {\n385\t                        let count_val = count.as_u64().unwrap_or(0);\n386\t                        *entry.entry(value.clone()).or_insert(0) += count_val;\n387\t                    }\n388\t                }\n389\t            }\n390\t        }\n391\t    }\n392\t\n393\t    // Convert back to JSON structure (BTreeMap ensures stable key order)\n394\t    let result: serde_json::Map<String, Value> = merged_facets\n395\t        .into_iter()\n396\t        .map(|(facet_name, values)| {\n397\t            let values_obj: serde_json::Map<String, Value> = values\n398\t                .into_iter()\n399\t                .map(|(k, v)| (k, Value::Number(v.into())))\n400\t                .collect();\n401\t            (facet_name, Value::Object(values_obj))\n402\t        })\n403\t        .collect();\n404\t\n405\t    Value::Object(result)\n406\t}\n407\t\n408\t/// Stub implementation that returns empty results.\n409\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n410\tpub struct StubMerger;\n411\t\n412\timpl Merger for StubMerger {\n413\t    fn merge(\n414\t        &self,\n415\t        _shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse>,\n416\t        _offset: usize,\n417\t        _limit: usize,\n418\t        _client_requested_score: bool,\n419\t    ) -> Result<MergedResult> {\n420\t        Ok(MergedResult {\n421\t            hits: Vec::new(),\n422\t            facets: serde_json::json!({}),\n423\t            total_hits: 0,\n424\t            processing_time_ms: 0,\n425\t            degraded: false,\n426\t        })\n427\t    }\n428\t}\n429\t\n430\t#[cfg(test)]\n431\tmod tests {\n432\t    use super::*;\n433\t\n434\t    fn create_hit(score: f64, id: &str) -> Value {\n435\t        serde_json::json!({\n436\t            \"id\": id,\n437\t            \"_rankingScore\": score,\n438\t            \"_miroir_shard\": 0,\n439\t        })\n440\t    }\n441\t\n442\t    fn create_shard_response(shard_id: u32, hits: Vec<Value>, total: u64) -> ShardResponse {\n443\t        ShardResponse {\n444\t            shard_id,\n445\t            body: serde_json::json!({\n446\t                \"hits\": hits,\n447\t                \"estimatedTotalHits\": total,\n448\t                \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n449\t                \"facetDistribution\": {},\n450\t            }),\n451\t            success: true,\n452\t        }\n453\t    }\n454\t\n455\t    #[test]\n456\t    fn test_global_sort_by_ranking_score() {\n457\t        let merger = MergerImpl;\n458\t\n459\t        let hits1 = vec![\n460\t            create_hit(0.5, \"doc1\"),\n461\t            create_hit(0.9, \"doc2\"),\n462\t            create_hit(0.3, \"doc3\"),\n463\t        ];\n464\t        let hits2 = vec![\n465\t            create_hit(0.7, \"doc4\"),\n466\t            create_hit(0.1, \"doc5\"),\n467\t            create_hit(0.8, \"doc6\"),\n468\t        ];\n469\t\n470\t        let shards = vec![\n471\t            create_shard_response(0, hits1, 3),\n472\t            create_shard_response(1, hits2, 3),\n473\t        ];\n474\t\n475\t        let result = merger.merge(shards, 0, 10, false).unwrap();\n476\t\n477\t        // Check global assert_eq!(result.hits[4][\"id\"], \"doc3\");\n484\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[5][\"id\"], \"doc5\");\n485\t    }\n486\t\n487\t    #[test]\n488\t    fn test_offset_and_limit_applied_after_merge() {\n489\t        let merger = MergerImpl;\n490\t\n491\t        let hits = vec![\n492\t            create_hit(0.9, \"doc1\"),\n493\t            create_hit(0.8, \"doc2\"),\n494\t vec![\n841\t            create_hit(0.5, \"zebra\"),\n842\t            create_hit(0.5, \"apple\"),\n843\t            create_hit(0.5, \"banana\"),\n844\t        ];\n845\t\n846\t        let shards =    }\n855\t\n856\t    #[test]\n857\t    fn test_stable_serialization_same_input_same_json() {\n858\t        let shard1 = serde_json::json!({\n859\t            \"hits\": [],\n860\t            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0,\n861\t            \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n862\t            \"facetDistribution\": {\n863\t                \"color\": {\"red\": 10, \"blue\": 5},\n864\t                \"size\": {\"large\": 8}\n865\t            }\n866\t        });\n867\t\n868\t        let shard2 = serde_json::json!({\n869\t            \"hits\": [],\n870\t            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0,\n871\t            \"processingTimeMs\": 15,\n872\t            \"facetDistribution\": {\n873\t                \"color\": {\"red\": 7, \"green\": 3},\n874\t                \"size\": {\"large\": 4, \"small\": 6}\n875\t            }\n876\t        });\n877\t\n878\t        let input = MergeInput {\n879\t            shard_hits: vec![\n880\t                ShardHitPage { body: shard1, success: true },\n881\t                ShardHitPage { body: shard2, success: true },\n882\t            ],\n883\t            offset: 0,\n884\t            limit: 10,\n885\t            client_requested_score: false,\n886\t            facets: None,\n887\t        };\n888\t\n889\t        let result1 = merge(input.clone());\n890\t        let result2 = merge(input);\n891\t\n892\t        // Serialize both results to JSON\n893\t        let json1 = serde_json::to_string(&result1).unwrap();\n894\t        let json2 = serde_json::to_string(&result2).unwrap();\n895\t\n896\t        // Must be byte-identical\n897\t        assert_eq!(json1, json2);\n898\t    }\n899\t\n900\t    #[test]\n901\t    fn test_binary_heap_efficiency_large_fan_out() {\n902\t        let merger = MergerImpl;\n903\t\n904\t        // Test that the false).unwrap();\n913\t\n914\t        // Should get the top 10 scores (0.90-0.99)\n915\t        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 10);\n916\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[0][\"id\"], \"doc99\"); // score 0.99\n917\t        assert_eq!(result.hits[9][\"id\"], \"doc90\"); // score 0.90\n918\t    }\n919\t\n920\t    #[test]\n921\t    fn test_offset_limit_pagination_reconstruction() {\n922\t        let        paged_ids.push(hit[\"id\"].as_str().unwrap().to_string());\n941\t            }\n942\t        }\n943\t\n944\t        // Concatenated pages should match the single limit=50 query\n945\t
